response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist software engineer with solid TypeScript/Node.js depth and genuine breadth across web, embedded, and cloud work, but he does not meet the core requirement of 8+ years building AI-driven solutions. His only explicit AI/agent work began in April 2025 at re:cinq, and the resume lacks any mention of Python, vector databases, RAG architectures, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.). The MCP work is promising and directly relevant, but it represents months of experience rather than the staff-level AI delivery track record this role demands.
strengths: Active contributor to MCP servers and agentic workflow infrastructure at current role, directly aligned with key technologies listed
Demonstrated staff-level seniority with a pattern of leading workshops, courses, and community knowledge-sharing across multiple employers
Long track record of high-throughput, production-grade systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby; AWS CDK pipelines at Optilyz)
concerns: Python is absent from the skills list entirely, yet it is the primary language called out in the job description for AI development
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or observability frameworks for AI systems
AI and agentic work is confined to the current role (April 2025–present), falling far short of the 8-year minimum for AI-driven solution delivery

key_factors.factor: Missing Python and RAG/vector DB skills
